answers: UFO stands for Unidentified Flying Object, now officially called UAP (Unidentified Aerial Phenomena) by the US government. These are aerial sightings that can't be immediately identified.

The US government has officially acknowledged UAP sightings by military pilots and released declassified footage. While this confirms unidentified objects exist in our skies, it doesn't necessarily mean they're alien spacecraft.

UFO mane Unidentified Flying Object, mane eamon kichu je akashe ure ar identify kora jay na. US government officially UAP (Unidentified Aerial Phenomena) bole. Area 51 holo ekta secret military base Nevada te.

UFOs have been reported worldwide for decades. The Roswell incident (1947), Phoenix Lights (1997), and recent US Navy pilot encounters are among the most famous. The government now takes these sightings more seriously and investigates through official channels.
